prompt.content:
Calculate in the model the 2030E net income attributable to S&P Global (consolidated entity) under the following assumptions:
•	Apply the midyear convention.
•	Apply 5.0% annual amortization of the outstanding beginning-of-year debt balance for the Senior Notes due 2033 for each forecast year 2026 through 2030.
•	Compute interest expense on the average of the beginning and ending debt balances for each year, multiplied by the applicable interest rate.
•	Set the interest rate for the Senior Notes due 2033 equal to the 1‑month U.S. Treasury yield as of December 31, 2025 plus a 2.0% pricing spread.
•	Switch the Senior Notes due 2037 to PIK interest payments.

Present the result in USD millions, rounded to the nearest dollar.

rubrics.name: States that 2030E net income attributable to S&P Global (consolidated entity)... (ver_76cd2cbb60c8477d9386229847b22f3b)

messages.content: States that 2030E net income attributable to S&P Global (consolidated entity) is $4,855 million (acceptable range $4,806 to $4,904).

golden_responses.golden_text_response: 2030E net income attributable to S&P Global (consolidated entity) is $4,855 million
